---
title: Munich Labor Market
---

The [Monatszahlen Arbeitsmarkt](https://opendata.muenchen.de/dataset/monatszahlen-arbeitsmarkt)
dataset, published monthly by the Statistisches Amt München, tracks
unemployment figures and open job postings for Munich going back to the 1990s.
It is one of the most watched economic indicators for the city.

Like the other Monatszahlen sources the portal publishes a single full-history
CSV that is replaced every month.  The pipeline fetches the current URL via the
Munich Open Data CKAN API, validates columns with
[`@schema`](../reference/schema.schema.qmd), and upserts into a Delta Lake
table using [`@incremental`](../reference/incremental.incremental.qmd).

**Data source:** Statistisches Amt München ·
License: [Datenlizenz Deutschland – Namensnennung 2.0](https://www.govdata.de/dl-de/by-2-0)

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
from pathlib import Path

import altair as alt
import polars as pl
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

BUCKET = os.environ.get("MUNICH_CYCLING_BUCKET", "data-warehousing-with-polars")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
df = (
    pl.scan_delta(f"s3://{BUCKET}/delta/munich_labor")
    .select("MONATSZAHL", "AUSPRAEGUNG", "JAHR", "WERT", "date")
    .collect()
    .sort("date")
)
df.head(5)
```

## Monthly unemployment

Total registered unemployed in Munich per month. The series reveals the
2008–2009 financial crisis, the COVID shock in spring 2020, and the subsequent
tight labour market.

```{python}
#| echo: false
unemployed = (
    df.filter(
        (pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Arbeitslose")
        & (pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG") == "insgesamt")
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
    .sort("date")
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("WERT").rolling_mean(window_size=12).alias("rolling_12m")
    )
)

base = alt.Chart(unemployed).encode(x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"))

raw = base.mark_line(opacity=0.25, color="#e45756").encode(
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Unemployed", axis=alt.Axis(format="~s")),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Unemployed", format=",.0f"),
    ],
)
smooth = base.mark_line(color="#e45756", strokeWidth=2).encode(
    y="rolling_12m:Q",
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("rolling_12m:Q", title="12-month avg", format=",.0f"),
    ],
)

(raw + smooth).properties(
    width="container", height=260, title="Total unemployed (Arbeitslose insgesamt)"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Unemployment by gender

Monthly unemployed broken down by gender, showing whether crises hit men and
women differently.

```{python}
#| echo: false
by_gender = (
    df.filter(
        (pl.col("MONATSZAHL") == "Arbeitslose")
        & (pl.col("AUSPRAEGUNG").is_in(["Männer", "Frauen"]))
    )
    .drop_nulls("WERT")
)

color_scale = alt.Scale(
    domain=["Männer", "Frauen"],
    range=["#4c78a8", "#e45756"],
)

selection = alt.selection_point(fields=["AUSPRAEGUNG"], bind="legend")

alt.Chart(by_gender).mark_line(strokeWidth=1.5).encode(
    x=alt.X("date:T", title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y("WERT:Q", title="Unemployed", axis=alt.Axis(format="~s")),
    color=alt.Color(
        "AUSPRAEGUNG:N",
        scale=color_scale,
        legend=alt.Legend(title="Gender"),
    ),
    opacity=alt.condition(selection, alt.value(1), alt.value(0.1)),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("date:T", title="Month", format="%b %Y"),
        alt.Tooltip("AUSPRAEGUNG:N", title="Gender"),
        alt.Tooltip("WERT:Q", title="Unemployed", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).add_params(selection).properties(
    width="container", height=260, title="Unemployed by gender"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```

## Seasonality

Average unemployment by calendar month across all available years, showing the
mild summer trough and winter uptick typical of Munich's labour market.

```{python}
#| echo: false
seasons = (
    unemployed
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("date").dt.month().alias("month_num"),
        pl.col("date").dt.strftime("%b").alias("month_name"),
    )
    .group_by("month_num", "month_name")
    .agg(pl.col("WERT").mean().alias("avg_unemployed"))
    .sort("month_num")
)

alt.Chart(seasons).mark_bar(color="#e45756").encode(
    x=alt.X("month_name:N", sort=None, title="Month"),
    y=alt.Y(
        "avg_unemployed:Q",
        title="Avg unemployed",
        axis=alt.Axis(format="~s"),
    ),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("month_name:N", title="Month"),
        alt.Tooltip("avg_unemployed:Q", title="Avg unemployed", format=",.0f"),
    ],
).properties(
    width="container", height=220, title="Average unemployment by month of year"
).configure_view(strokeWidth=0)
```
